rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type scopes for assumptions/guarantees and implementation scopes for bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when generating AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annexes in valid scopes without introducing new package references.
Do not encode AADL properties like Period, Deadline, or Priority as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
Avoid unsafe Boolean or numeric comparisons on event ports or opaque data types without explicit model support.
Do not introduce new identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions and guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties as AGREE variables unless they are explicitly typed ports or constants in scope.
Do not assume event ports or opaque data can be encoded as numeric or Boolean values without model support.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and structure in component implementations
Model validation diagnostics and repair patterns
AADL property associations and their mapping to AGREE expressions
